remember: Students will be able to recall the Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction.
Students will be able to state Lenz's law and its significance.
Students will be able to identify the types of electromagnetic induction.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of induced emf and current.
Students will be able to explain the working of a generator and motor.
Students will be able to describe the conditions necessary for electromagnetic induction.
apply: Students will be able to apply Faraday's laws to determine the direction of induced current.
Students will be able to use Lenz's law to predict the direction of induced emf.
Students will be able to design a simple electric generator.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors affecting electromagnetic induction.
Students will be able to compare the advantages and disadvantages of different types of electromagnetic induction.
Students will be able to critique the design of a simple electric motor.

ncert_objectives: Explain the phenomenon of electromagnetic induction and describe the factors on which the induced e.m.f. depends.
State and explain Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction.
Describe Lenz's law and explain its significance.
Explain the principle and working of a generator and a motor.
board_exam_objectives: Explain electromagnetic induction and describe the factors on which the induced e.m.f. depends.
State and explain Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction and Lenz's law.
Describe the working of a generator and a motor, and explain the conditions necessary for electromagnetic induction.
Derive an expression for the induced e.m.f. in a coil and describe the factors affecting electromagnetic induction.
